source_notes_json: ["This is an in vivo dermatopharmacokinetic (DPK) tape-strip study, not a classical Franz-cell IVPT/IVRT. No receptor medium or diffusion cell is used. Barrier is human stratum corneum in vivo. Endpoint is SC drug content (µg/cm²) at various time points post-formulation removal (clearance phase). Diffusion area estimated from template diameter of 2.5 cm (π×1.25²≈4.91 cm²). Initial uptake amount at time=0 post-removal from Figure 3 text is 12.2 ± 3.5 µg/cm²; Table II reports 13.6 ± 4.1 for delay=0. Both values are from separate subject sets but same protocol."]

source_notes_json: ["Abstract only available. Study used Franz cells to assess ibuprofen (and caffeine) permeation through acetone-treated vs. untreated skin. Abstract states 'very low permeation' of ibuprofen into deeper skin layers after acetone treatment, but no numeric endpoints, formulation details, receptor medium, dose, or area are reported in the available content. Full paper is required for quantitative extraction.", "needs_supp=yes"]
